"WHACKING DAY" by John Swartzwelder ACT ONE Opening credits couch gag sequence will be an extended production numer as DANCERS, ELEPHANTS and JUGGLERS pour into the room after the family runs in. FADE IN: SCENE 1 INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - LISA’S CLASS - DAY The classroom is a mess: wads of paper strewn about, apple cores and banana peels on the floor, etc. Principal Skinner comes on the loudspeaker. SKINNER (V.0.) Children, we’ve just been tipped off that Superintendent Chalmers is planning a surprise inspection. So let’s clean up this pigsty. LISA Maybe we can make a game of cleaning up. MISS HOOVER No. Just do it. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - BART’S CLASS - DAY SKINNER (V.0) I want every student to clean out his

or her desk.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

BART opens his desk. A giant mound of garbage surges up -- crumpled papers, apple cores, old sandwiches (etc.). A couple of SEAGULLS see this through the window -- they fly in and pick among the garbage. Bart tries to shoo them off. One seagull SNAPS at him. SEAGULL (ANGRY SQUAWK) SKINNER (V.0.) (OVER PA) On another topic, the following students have won mountain bikes: Bart Simpson... Bart and MRS. KRABAPPEL look shocked. ANGLE - HALLWAY

We see JIMBO stealing the P.A. speaker the announcement is coming from. There are other P.A. speakers in a bag.

SKINNER (CONT’D) Jimbo Jones... ANGLE

We see NELSON holding MILHOUSE in a headlock as he stuffs grass and twigs into Milhouse’s shirt.

SKINNER (CONT’D) Nelson Muntz... He turns to Milhouse. NELSON (CONT’D) I want you to keep filling your shirt with crud till I get back. MILHOUSE (SAD) Yes, sir.

He continues to do so as Nelson runs off.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - SKINNER’S OFFICE - DAY Skinner is at the P.A. SKINNER You may pick your bikes up in Utility Basement B. He puts the microphone down. SKINNER (CONT’D) Fools! (STARTS AN EVIL LAUGH, TURNS, STOPS SHORT) Whoops. Still on. He shuts off the Mic. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - CORRIDOR = DAY SCENE 2 Jimbo, DOLPH, KEARNEY, Nelson, and Bart approach a rusty door marked: "Civil Defense Shelter." Under it is a sign eeedins “Mountain Bikes in Here." Bart looks up at the BART Hey, what do you think he meant by that fools remark? NELSON Ah, who cares? It’s time to get me a mountain bike! They open the door and step inside. INT. CIVIL DEFENSE SHELTER - CONTINUOUS

They turn on the light, revealing a cobwebbed room with old crates marked "Water" and "Crackers."

NELSON Hey, what gives? Where are the mountain bikes?

Suddenly, Skinner pokes his head in.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 4.

SKINNER Sorry about the ruse, gentlemen. You’re being swept under the rug for the Superintendent’s visit. Enjoy!

He SLAMS the door closed. We hear the door being BOLTED and LOCKED.

JIMBO How are we gonna get outta here? NELSON And when are we gonna get our mountain bikes? EXT. DEFENSE SHELTER - CONTINUOUS Skinner gloats with WILLIE. SKINNER Would the world judge me harshly if I threw away the key? GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE No. But the PTA would tear ya a new arse. SKINNER Wise council, William. But the potty talk adds nothing. GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE (MUTTERED) Aye, sir. (MUTTERING) You bath-taking, underpants-wearing, lily-

hugger.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 5.

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - CAFETERIA - DAY

LUNCHLADY DORIS is supervising the hanging of a banner that says: "Welcome Superintendent Chalmers."

LUNCHLADY DORIS Hang that banner higher. Mr. Chalmers isn’t a midget! Higher! Higher! They raise it an inch higher. LUNCHLADY DORIS (CONT’D) Lower. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - DAY SCENE 3 CHALMERS enters and Skinner greets hin. SKINNER Superintendent Chalmers! Welcome! CHALMERS Hello, Seymour. SKINNER So what’s the word down at One School Board Plaza? CHALMERS We’re dropping the geography requirement. The children weren’t testing well. It was proving to be an embarrassment. SKINNER Very good. Back to the three R’s. CHALMERS

(CONFIDENTIALLY) Two R’s come October.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

Skinner and Chalmers walk down the hallways. Banners read: “Welcome Superintendent," and "We Love Chalmie."

SKINNER (LEADING) What do you think of the banners? CHALMERS Nothing but transparent toadying. SKINNER They were the children’s idea. I tried to stop then. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - CIVIL DEFENSE SHELTER

Bart and the other troublemakers are sitting on the floor glumly.

NELSON Imagine, a school out there with no bullies.

JIMBO Science geeks not getting beat up... kids using their lunch money for food...

NELSON I can’t take it!

He starts PUNCHING the wall.

BART Hey, I think I can squeeze through that vent.

JIMBO

Alright!

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

NELSON Alright Bart! Do it man.

Jimbo helps Bart into the vent. JIMBO Now, remember little dude, you’re our

only hope.

Nelson whips a rock down the vent at Bart.

BART (V.0.) ow!

NELSON Got ‘im!

JIMBO

Way to go, man! NELSON Haw Haw! INT. CAFETERIA - DAY Skinner is leading Chalmers through. SKINNER May I interest you in a jello brick, Sir? (CONSPIRATORIAL) There’s a grape in the center. CHALMERS Well, I’m not made of stone.

He takes the jello brick. They sit at a table. Chalmers takes a bite of meatloaf.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 8.

CHALMERS (CONT’D) Hm. This meatloaf tastes rather grainy. You haven’t been stretching it with low-cost meat extenders, have you? SKINNER Oh no, Sir. Even the Sloppy Joes are neat as a pin. INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS Lunchlady Doris and her assistant, Gert are pouring bags labelled "SAWDUST" and "CORK" into the Sloppy Joe mix. A male assistant comes up and empties a pencil sharpener in the mix.

EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - WILLIE’S SHACK - DAY SCENE 4

GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE is adjusting the idle on his tractor, wearing a full dress kilt.

GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE Ach, my beauty, wait till the Superintendent sees you. Were it not a violation of God’s law, I’d make ye my wife.

Willie kisses the tractor.

SCOTTISH WOMAN (0.S.)

Now there’s a lonely man.

A startled Willie looks up to see a pretty SCOTTISH WOMAN. We hear:

MUSIC: "LOVE AT FIRST SIGHT" SCOTTISH WOMAN (COYLY) I’m new in town. Be there a cool loch where a lass could wash her

long red hair?

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 9.

GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE Nay, but there’s a pool at me apartment complex. There was a rat in the deep end -- but we got him! SCOTTISH WOMAN Lead on. The two walk off arm in arm. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - LISA’S CLASSROOM - DAY Chalmers is counting the stars on the flag. CHALMERS Forty-eight... forty-nine... fifty. Flag’s up to date. Very good, Seymour. (SURVEYING STUDENTS) Now, are these children as smart as they look? SKINNER Well let’s pick one at random. How about that one? Skinner indicates Lisa. CHALMERS (POINTS TO RALPH) You mean this boy, here? SKINNER (HORRIFIED) No! No! Lisa Simpson! CHALMERS (TO LISA) When was the battle of New

Orleans?

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

LISA

January 8, 1815. Two weeks after the

war ended.

CHALMERS First rate.

RALPH What’s a battle?

SKINNER (LAUGHS TOO HARD) Let’s go.

CHALMERS

Did that boy say what’s a battle?

SKINNER

No he said what’s that rattle. (UNDER

HIS BREATH) It’s about the heating

duct.

EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - WILLIE’S SHACK = DAY SCENE 5

A dirty, disheveled Bart runs past the tractor which is idling unattended. After a beat, Bart comes back into frame and stares at the tractor thoughtfully.

The lid on the tractor’s exhaust pipe FLAPS up and down

like a mouth. EXHAUST PIPE LID Come on Bart. Ride me. BART (LOOKING AROUND) I better not.

The Exhaust Pipe Lid CLUCKS like a chicken. down. He sees a chicken.

Bart looks

10.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

CHICKEN (SNOOTY VOICE) He’s insulting both of us.

BART (TO TRACTOR) Let's go.

Bart hops aboard and steps on the gas. The tractor ZOOMS off, throwing Bart back in his seat.

BART (CONT’D)

(SCREAMS) EXT. SCHOOL YARD - CONTINUOUS We see the runaway tractor cutting a swath of destruction. It flattens a row of bikes and then ROARS through a swing set, narrowly missing each swinging child. Bart looks terrified as the tractor races up and around a curved baseball backstop.

BART

(DOPPLER YELL)

EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. YARD - DAY

Chalmers takes a pinch of sand from the sandbox and tastes it.

CHALMERS Excellent. Not a trace of urine.

Chalmers throws a friendly arm around Skinner. They start to stroll.

CHALMERS (CONT’D) Seymour, you run a tight ship. SKINNER Well, you know what they say, sir. "Where there’s no smoke, there’s no

fire."

11.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

CHALMERS What an odd remark. We hear Bart’s TRACTOR approaching in the distance. CHALMERS (CONT’D) (SEES SOMETHING ON GROUND) Why, that looks like a fifty cent piece. I/11 just bend over and get it. Chalmers bends over. ANGLE ON BART He’s hanging on for dear life as the tractor heads for Chalmers. The Superintendent’s rear end makes an inviting target. ANGLE ON CHALMERS As he struggles with the half dollar. CHALMERS (CONT’D) (TO SELF) It seems to be caught between these two flowers. ANGLE ON BART He realizes a collision is imminent and leaps off the tractor. Skinner reacts too late and the tractor plows into Chalmer’s backside with a horrible MOWING SOUND. SKINNER (SCREAMS) NOOOOO! EXT. BONNIE BRAE - DAY Groundskeeper Willie hears Skinner’s CRY and jerks up from a torrid embrace. His face is covered with lipstick. A woman’s hand reaches up and pulls him back down.

SCOTTISH WOMAN (V.0.)

‘Tis only bog gas.

12.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 13.

INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEM. - NURSE’S OFFICE - LATER

Lunchlady Doris is bandaging a furious Chalmers. A worried Bart and Skinner look on.

CHALMERS Ow, Ow, ow. Seymour I was thinking of promoting you to Assistant Superintendent. But, instead, that plum goes to Holloway. SKINNER No but sir, he’s a drunk. CHALMERS, And a pill popper. SKINNER If I could just... CHALMERS Silence! And why is a cafeteria worker posing as a nurse? LUNCHLADY DORIS I get two paychecks this way. CHALMERS, (ANNOYED GRUNT) Skinner turns to Bart. SKINNER (COLD FURY) There’s no detention this time, Simpson. This is the end. You are expelled from Springfield Elementary!

Bart looks out the window at Willie’s tractor.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 14.

EXHAUST PIPE LID (LONG MANIACAL LAUGH) The top section of the exhaust pipe falls off. EXHAUST PIPE LID (CONT’D) (UNHAPPY NOISE)

FADE OUT:

END OF ACT ONE

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

FADE IN: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY SCENE 6 Lisa and Bart are watching TV. ON TV We see KENT BROCKMAN standing in a field. KENT BROCKMAN Tonight on "Eye on Springfield": The Munchkins from "The Wizard of Oz"... Where are they now? We see a shot of a cemetery. KENT BROCKMAN (CONT’D) And we’ll visit a nudist camp... for animals!

We see a DOG and a CAT walking around with black bars over their private parts.

KENT BROCKMAN (CONT’D) But first, a look at the local holiday that made Newsweek’s "10 Blights on America" list... (GRINNING) Whacking Day! INT. SIMPSON HOUSE = LIVING ROOM = CONTINUOUS LISA Oh no. No! ON TV

We see the following:

15.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 16.

KENT BROCKMAN (V.0O.) In a tradition that dates back to founding father Jebediah Springfield, every May 10th local residents gather to drive snakes into the center of town and whack them to Snake Heaven. We see old newsreel footage of SPRINGFIELDITES gathered around the perimeter of town, then marching towards the center of the town BANGING on pots and pans. Snakes flee in front of them. When they get to the center, the men start WHACKING all the snakes, although we do not see snakes getting killed. The footage changes to a YOUNG RICHARD NIXON awkwardly wielding a stick and trying to brain a snake that his secret servicemen have pinned to the ground. SUPER: 1949 KENT BROCKMAN (V.0O.) After exposing Alger Hiss, honorary Grand Marshall Richard Nixon goes after another deadly hiss. Nixon takes careful aim and repeatedly WHACKS the secret serviceman who is holding the snake down. Finally the stick is taken away from Nixon and he is led away. NIXON (CONFUSED) Is Whacking Day over? CROWD (BOOS) NIXON Thank you, thanks for coming out.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS SCENE 7

Bart and Lisa are on the couch.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

LISA (SHUDDERS) It’s all so barbaric. Homer enters. HOMER Hey kids. How was school? LISA I learned how many drams in a pennyweight. BART I got expelled. HOMER That’s my boy. (HALF A BEAT) What? BART Look, Dad. I don’t need school. I/11 make my way as a bootblack. (COCKNEY ACCENT) Shine yer boots Guv‘nor? Me dad’s been hanged and me mum’s gone starkers. HOMER No son of mine’s gonna be a 19th Century Cockney bootblack. We’re gonna find you another school. EXT. RELIGIOUS SCHOOL - ESTABLISHING

A sign reads "SPRINGFIELD CHRISTIAN SCHOOL - WE PUT THE ‘FUN’ IN FUNDAMENTALIST DOGMA"

INT. RELIGIOUS SCHOOL

Bart is in a one-room religious school. It’s EZEKIEL and ISHMAEL’S class. MARGE takes Bart aside.

17.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

MARGE (LOW) Now remember Bart. This is a religious school. So be very careful

about what you say and do here.

BART No problemo. Marge exits. Bart takes his seat. The teacher, MR. OGDEN, addresses hin. MR. OGDEN

Now Bart, since you’re new here, perhaps you’d favor us with a psalm. BART How about "Beans, Beans, the Musical Fruit?" MR. OGDEN (PUZZLED) Well, beans were a staple of the Israelites...yes, proceed. BART Beans, beans, the musical fruit/ The more you eat the more you toot... EXT. RELIGIOUS SCHOOL - CONTINUOUS Bart is backing out of the school. The teacher tries to SMITE Bart with a paddle. Bart picks up a plastic sheep from a nativity scene and fends him off with it. MR. OGDEN Avert your eyes, children, he may take on other forms!

Marge looks over from her car.

18.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

MARGE (SIGHS) Oh, Bart. EXT. SPRINGFIELD MILITARY ACADEMY - ESTABLISHING SCENE 8 INT. ACADEMY - CONTINUOUS

A kindly old GENERAL stands over Bart, who sits ina uniform.

GENERAL Now, all you have to do is sign this form promising you won’t use your military training for treasonous purposes. BART Blah blah blah. GENERAL Bart, it’s just a formality saying you won’t kill the President or over-throw the government. BART I know what it says. The general reacts. EXT. MILITARY SCHOOL - LATER THAT DAY Marge looks over from her car to see the General RIPPING off Bart’s epaulets and breaking his sword over his knee. A drummer PLAYS. MARGE (SIGHS) Oh, Bart. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - DINING ROOM

The family is eating dinner.

19.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92

BART

I guess that’s it. I just can’t be

educated. (SIGHS) I can always get a

job testing dangerous food additives.

BART’S FANTASY

An adult, shabby looking Bart sits at a table in a lab. TWO SCIENTISTS approach Bart carrying a tray with a soda

can.

SCIENTIST #1

We’d like you to try this new diet

cola. We call it "Nature’s Goodness."

BART What‘’s in it? SCIENTIST #2

2,4 des-oxy-propheniramine.

Bart drinks the soda, and immediately metamorphoses into a

MR. HYDE-TYPE of character. BART (MONSTER-LIKE) Sweet. One scientist makes notes. SCIENTIST #2 Pleasing taste. Some monsterism. BACK TO SCENE BART Cool. MARGE You’re going to get an education.

going to teach you myself.

I‘n

20.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 21.

HOMER Marge, it’s too late. The boy’s ten years old! Let’s focus our energy on Lisa and the other one. Homer looks over at Lisa. She is morose. HOMER (CONT’D) What‘’s your problem? LISA (SULLEN) Whacking Day is coming. HOMER Woo hoo! The greatest day of the year. LISA But killing snakes is so cruel. HOMER (PATIENT) Oh Lisa, snakes are evil creatures that can (SCARY) hypnotize you. And if you cut off one’s head, it lives until sunset. It’s all in this pamphlet.

He pulls a pamphlet out of his hip pocket and shows it to Lisa.

LISA (READING) "Common Misconceptions About Snakes." Dad, do you know what a misconception is?

HOMER (SNIDELY) No I don’t know what a

misconception is.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 22.

EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - ESTABLISHING INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - MORNING AFTER SCENE 9 Lisa is finishing putting on her coat and grabbing her lunch box. Bart is still in his PJ’s, lounging on the couch. BART Have a nice day at school, Lis. I/11 just try to make the best of a bad situation. (GETTING COMFORTABLE NOISE) Ahhh... Bart lies back on the couch dreamily. Marge enters. MARGE Bart. Get dressed for school. I expect you to be in your seat when the bell rings. Bart and Lisa look at her. MARGE (CONT’D) (GIGGLES) I bought a bell. INT. GARAGE - 9 AM Marge has turned the garage into a makeshift classroom. There’s a cat clock from the kitchen and a large school bell on the wall, a blackboard, a desk for Marge with a little flag and a plastic apple on it, and a desk for Bart. Bart is in his seat, looking around curiously, when the school bell on the wall GOES OFF. It sounds like the end of the world. Both he and Marge SCREAM. MARGE (RECOVERING) I think I/1l unplug that.

Marge writes her name carefully on the blackboard.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 23.

MARGE (CONT’D) I’m your new teacher. My name is Mrs. Simpson (GIGGLES). Bart LAUGHS politely. INT. GARAGE - 11 AM Marge is writing on the blackboard. Suddenly Bart makes a flurry of GROSS NOISES and throws a paper airplane which sticks in Marge’s hair. She turns and looks at hin. BART I didn’t do it. Marge keeps looking at him. MARGE (MURMUR) INT. GARAGE - 2 PM Marge is sitting at her desk watching Bart as he writes out the answers to math problems on the board. The doorbell RINGS. MARGE Oh, that’s right. I invited a guest speaker to talk to the class. GRAMPA Hello, children! INT. GARAGE - A LITTLE LATER Grampa is telling a story to an intrigued Bart. GRAMPA I got separated from my platoon after we parachuted into Dusseldorf. So I

rode out the war posing as a German

cabaret singer.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 24.

INT. 40’8 STYLE NIGHT CLUB A LA THE MOVIE "CABARET" We see a 40’s-style chorus line of FEMALE DANCERS. Suddenly they part to reveal Grampa, also dressed as a female dancer. Grampa is singing to the tune of "Won’t you come home Bill Bailey?" GRAMPA (SINGING) Won’t you come home Franz

Brauder / Won’t you come home...

In the audience, HITLER makes goo goo eyes at Grampa and waves alluringly.

HITLER Yoo hoo.

Grampa responds by lowering one of the straps from his dress. Suddenly, a melon falls out of his brassiere cup.

HITLER (CONT’D) Ach du lieber! Eine melon! BACK TO SCENE BART (SKEPTICAL) Is that story true, Grampa? GRAMPA Well, most of it. I did wear a dress for a period in the forties. (RAPTUROUSLY) Oh, they had designers then...

Suddenly the garage door opens and Homer drives into the schoolroon.

BART/MARGE/GRAMPA (SCREAMS) INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER SCENE 10

Homer is holding a bag with the words "SNAKES" emblazoned on it with rhinestones.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 25.

HOMER Look what I got, Marge. A new whacking stick. Homer mimes whacking snakes. HOMER (CONT’D) Whack! MARGE (TITILLATING) Oooh! LISA (REPULSED) Excuse me. She exits. HOMER Whack! Whack! Whack! MARGE Oooh! Oooooh! Ooooo00h! INT. GARAGE - BART’S CLASS - NEXT DAY Bart looks a little more attentive. Marge holds up a book. MARGE Bart, I/d like you to read this copy of "Johnny Tremain." It’s a book I read as a girl. BART A book? (HE MAKES A DISGUSTED NOISE) MARGE I think you might like this... It’s about a boy who goes to war... his hand

is deformed in an accident...

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 26.

BART

(SUDDENLY INTERESTED) Deformed? Why

didn’t you say so? (TAKING BOOK) They

should call this book "Johnny

Deformed!" INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT ON TV We see an Itchy and Scratchy Cartoon. SCRATCHY is being led through the basement of a police station a la Lee Harvey Oswald. ITCHY approaches like Jack Ruby. He SHOOTS Scratchy. Freeze on Scratchy’s anguished face as we PAN

over and see Bart has his nose buried in the Johnny Tremain book.

ON TV We hear "Oh God!" and "Get his gun." Bart doesn’t look up. INT. GARAGE - NEXT MORNING SCENE 11

Bart is reading the last page of the book aloud. Marge is sitting on his desk.

BART “Hundreds would die, but not the thing they died for: ‘A man can stand up.../" Wow. MARGE Mm hmm. If you’d like to learn more about Colonial times we could take a field trip to Old Springfield Towne. BART I thought they closed that old

firetrap.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 27.

MARGE (NICELY) Not until June. Homer drives into the classroom again. HOMER (ANNOYED GRUNT) EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE Lisa watches sadly as Homer leaps about the yard swinging his stick. He spins, twirls and WHACKS with lightning speed in all directions. He has the whole yard booby- trapped with false snakes. They jump out at him and SCREECH, like at the Disneyland Haunted House. HOMER (KUNG FU NOISES) Homer flips end over end across the yard, WHACKING the false snakes as they jump out at him. He spins, and KICKS the garden hose out into the street. LISA (TRYING TO GET HIS ATTENTION) Dad...Dad? He almost hits her. HOMER Careful, I thought you were a snake. LISA Dad, everyone likes Whacking Day, but I

hate it. Is there something wrong with

me?

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 28.

HOMER (GENTLY) Yes, honey. Because you see, the majority is always right. Whether it’s their love for disco or, a year later, their hate for disco.

LISA So, what should I do?

HOMER Just squeeze your rage into a bitter little ball and release it at an appropriate time. Like that day I hit that referee with a whiskey bottle. Remember that?

Lisa looks worried.

LISA Yeah.

HOMER

Your Daddy hit the referee.

LISA Yeah. HOMER Yeah. EXT. OLD SPRINGFIELD TOWNE SCENE 12 The sign reads: "Old Springfield Towne." Below this a

sign reads "WANTED: VILLAGE IDIOT." A RECRUITER looks at an actor’s head shot.

A) Marge and Bart walk up to Barney who’s dressed in Colonial garb and passed out on the ground with a bottle beside him. MARGE Hello, Barney. Are you playing the town drunk? BARNEY Actually, I’m supposed to be the governor. (BELCH) B) Bart and Marge are standing in front of a stockade, listening to the GUIDE telling the story of a famous battle. GUIDE The enemy surrounded the fort and said that if the captain was sent out the rest would be spared. BART What did they do? GUIDE

They sent him out.

BART Was he killed?

GUIDE And how! And everyone else in the fort lived to be a hundred. That’s why they call it Fort Sensible.

TEENAGED GUIDE This is Jebediah Springfield at the Battle of Ticonderoga. Next. (NEXT SLIDE) And here he is killing a snake on the very first Whacking Day in 1775. Bart raises his hand. BART (CALLING) Hey wait a minute. That was the same day he was at Ticonderoga. How could he be in two places at once? TEENAGED GUIDE Uh... Um... Y¥/see... Behind Bart we see a diorama of PAUL REVERE. outta here.

BOY TENOR (SINGING) We’1l break their backs/ Gouge out their eyes/ Their evil hearts we'll pulverize... CHILDREN’S CHOIR (SINGING) Oh Whacking Day/ Oh Whacking Day/ May God bestow His grace on thee. HOMER (CONT’D) Coming boy?

Bart is on the floor reading a book entitled "The Truth About Whacking Day" by Bob Woodward.

BART Nah... I’d rather finish this book. HOMER (STERN) All right, but tonight you are watching two hours of network TV. EXT. SPRINGFIELD CITY LIMITS - LATER SCENE 15 A few SNAKES peek out of the bushes, looking apprehensive.

Quimby steps to the podium.

QUIMBY And now, to open this year’s festivities, here’s our Grand Marshall, (READING CARD) "The Prophet of Love"... Larry White! BARRY WHITE Barry White. QUIMBY (CHECKING CARD) No, it says here, Larry White. BARRY WHITE I know my own name. QUIMBY Yeah, well, we’1ll see. Barry White steps to the podium amidst APPLAUSE. BARRY WHITE Ladies and Gentlemen, my unlimited love to y’all. It’s truly an honor to be here at this... (TO QUIMBY) ... hey, what is this all about anyway? Quimby WHISPERS something to hin. There’s a CHEER and the MEN begin BANGING pots and pans together, moving towards the center of town. We see OTTO surfing on a wave of snakes. A wave wipes him out. He stands and looks at the receding wave. oTTo

Whoa! Man, I was at one with the snakes. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE Bart is still reading his book. Lisa is sitting on the couch, miserable. She can hear the BANGING of the pots and pans in the distance. She covers her ears. LISA

If the snakes were in here we could

protect them.

Oh, good idea! Let’s see... bass,

bass... "Tiny Tim"... "The Chipmunks Greatest Hits"... "A Castrato Christmas"... (FRUSTRATED NOISE)

love, babe. Well I don’t know... I

don’t know why... can’t get enough of

We see MR. LARGO is sitting on a park bench. A lady bug lands on his shoulder.

SIMPSON HOUSE - CONTINUOUS The mob starts to advance. Bart addresses the crowd. BART People of Springfield! Whacking Day is a sham! He holds up his book. BART (CONT’D) It was started in 1924 as an excuse to beat up the Irish. CROWD (MURMUR) An old IRISHMAN steps out of the crowd. IRISHMAN ‘Tis true! I took many a lump, but ‘twas all in good fun. Homer steps forward. HOMER Don’t listen to the boy! He’s just a rabble... quieter.

Lisa addresses the crowd.

9F18 FINAL DELIVERY 9/25/92 43.

LISA Now wait a minute. How can you people turn on snakes after all they’ve done for you?

GRAMPA Oh snakes are only out for themselves, just like anyone born after 1951.

LISA Oh yeah? Mrs. Glick, who killed all those rats in your basement?

OLD MRS. GLICK Snake did.

BART Mr. Burns, what did you put in that investigative reporter’s mailbox?

MR. BURNS A snake. But, how did you know?

BART We/’ll talk later. And you like snakes, don’t you Barry White?

BARRY WHITE I love the sexy slither of a lady snake. Oh baby.

Quimby’s limousine pulls up. He gets out holding a dozen dead snakes.
